enrichment.short_description: Gravitee is an API management platform with AI agent governance, event streaming security, and a developer portal.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Gravitee: API management platform with AI agent governance, event streaming, and gateway plans from $2,500/month.

features.title: AI Agent & MCP Governance
features.description: Secures and governs AI agents and Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers with identity/access control, LLM cost and rate limiting, and PII filtering.

features.title: Multi-Gateway API Management
features.description: Deploys API gateways across multiple brokers with observability, governance, and a self-service developer portal.

features.title: Kafka & Event Stream Security
features.description: Exposes and secures Kafka streams with protocol mediation for event-driven architectures.

features.title: Open Source API Gateway
features.description: Includes an open-source API Gateway component alongside its commercial API management platform.

features.title: API Observability
features.description: Provides API observability and governance tooling across gateway deployments.

pros: Recognized as a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for API Management for two consecutive years
Includes an open-source API Gateway option
Covers API, event stream (Kafka), and AI agent/MCP governance in one platform
Used by large enterprises including Walmart, BMW, Michelin, and Schneider Electric
cons: Entry-level API Management plan (Planet) starts at $2,500/month
Higher tiers (Galaxy, Universe) require custom/contact-sales pricing
No free tier pricing disclosed for the full commercial platform beyond the OSS gateway

faqs.question: Is Gravitee open source?
faqs.answer: Gravitee offers an open-source API Gateway component in addition to its commercial API management, event management, and AI agent governance platform.

faqs.question: How much does Gravitee cost?
faqs.answer: Gravitee's API Management starts at $2,500/month for the Planet plan; Event Management starts at $1,250/month for the Comet plan. Higher tiers require custom pricing.

faqs.question: Does Gravitee support AI agent governance?
faqs.answer: Yes, Gravitee includes AI Agent Management features such as MCP server governance, LLM cost/rate controls, and agent-to-agent traffic auditing.

faqs.question: What does Gravitee do with event streams?
faqs.answer: Gravitee can expose and secure Kafka streams with protocol mediation for event-driven architectures.

use_cases.title: Enterprise API Management
use_cases.description: Manage, secure, and observe APIs across gateways with a developer portal.
use_cases.audience: Platform and API teams at large enterprises

use_cases.title: AI Agent Governance
use_cases.description: Control identity, access, and cost for AI agents and MCP servers.
use_cases.audience: Teams deploying AI agents and LLM-based systems

use_cases.title: Event Stream Security
use_cases.description: Secure and mediate Kafka event streams across brokers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Gravitee?
paragraphs: Gravitee is an API and AI agent management platform that secures, governs, and controls APIs, event streams, and AI agents from a single system. It combines API gateway and management functionality with AI agent governance features such as MCP server control and LLM cost/rate limiting.

The platform includes an open-source API Gateway component alongside commercial API Management, Event Management, and Agent Management add-ons. Gravitee has been recognized as a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for API Management for two consecutive years and is used by enterprises including Walmart, BMW, Michelin, and Schneider Electric.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Gravitee is for
paragraphs: Gravitee suits platform and API teams at mid-size to large enterprises that need to manage APIs, Kafka event streams, and AI agents/MCP servers under one governance model, and that can accommodate its enterprise-oriented pricing starting at $2,500/month.

enrichment.short_description: KrakenD is a high-performance, stateless open-source API gateway with a paid Enterprise Edition for SLAs and support.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: KrakenD: open-source, stateless API gateway handling 80K+ req/sec, with an Enterprise Edition offering SLAs and support.

features.title: API Aggregation
features.description: Combines multiple backend responses into a single API response.

features.title: Traffic Management
features.description: Provides rate limiting, circuit breakers, bot detection, and IP filtering.

features.title: Authentication & Security
features.description: Supports JWT, OAuth2, OpenID, API keys, mTLS, and Basic Auth.

features.title: Multi-Protocol Support
features.description: Works with REST, GraphQL, gRPC, RabbitMQ, Kafka, SNS, SQS, and Lambda.

features.title: AI Gateway Capabilities
features.description: Adds LLM routing, prompt guardrails, token budgets, and an MCP server.

features.title: Observability
features.description: Exports logs, metrics, and traces via OpenTelemetry.

features.title: GitOps-Ready Configuration
features.description: Version-controlled, single-binary configuration deployable via GitOps workflows.

pros: Community Edition is free and open source, distributed as a single binary
Handles 80K+ requests/second on commodity hardware per vendor benchmarks
Broad protocol and authentication support (REST, GraphQL, gRPC, JWT, OAuth2, mTLS)
SOC 2 Type II certified
Stateless architecture avoids the need for a database or external state store
cons: Enterprise Edition pricing is not published; requires contacting sales
24/7 support and SLAs are limited to the paid Enterprise Edition

faqs.question: Is KrakenD free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the KrakenD Community Edition is free and open source. A paid Enterprise Edition adds SLAs, 24/7 support, and additional governance features.

faqs.question: What protocols does KrakenD support?
faqs.answer: KrakenD supports REST, GraphQL, gRPC, and messaging systems including RabbitMQ, Kafka, SNS, SQS, and AWS Lambda.

faqs.question: Does KrakenD require a database?
faqs.answer: No, KrakenD is a stateless gateway distributed as a single binary with no external state store required.

faqs.question: Where is KrakenD based?
faqs.answer: KrakenD is made by KRAKEND S.L.U., based in Barcelona, Spain.

use_cases.title: Microservices API Aggregation
use_cases.description: Aggregate multiple microservice responses into a single API call.

use_cases.title: AI/LLM Gateway
use_cases.description: Route and govern LLM traffic with prompt guardrails and token budgets.

use_cases.title: Enterprise API Gateway
use_cases.description: Deploy a production-grade gateway with SLA-backed support.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is KrakenD?
paragraphs: KrakenD is a high-performance, stateless API gateway used to connect, secure, aggregate, transform, and filter data across microservices and AI workloads. It ships as a single binary with no runtime dependencies and is built by KRAKEND S.L.U. in Barcelona, Spain.

The open-source Community Edition is free to use and can reportedly handle over 80,000 requests per second on commodity hardware. A paid Enterprise Edition adds SLA-backed support, advanced governance, and unlimited requests/APIs at flat pricing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who KrakenD is for
paragraphs: KrakenD fits backend and platform teams building microservice or AI/LLM architectures that need a fast, stateless gateway, and organizations that want the option to move from a free open-source edition to a supported enterprise deployment.

name: Gloo Gateway
skip_reason: Rebranded/discontinued under this name: Solo.io donated the Gloo Gateway open-source project to the CNCF and renamed it 'kgateway'; starting with version 2.1 the commercial product is marketed as 'Solo Enterprise for kgateway'. The solo.io/products/gloo-gateway page itself now refers to the product as Kgateway, so 'Gloo Gateway' no longer matches the current, live product name.

enrichment.short_description: Traefik Hub is a Kubernetes-native API management platform from Traefik Labs, built on open-source Traefik Proxy.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Traefik Hub: Kubernetes-native API management platform with GitOps workflows, built on open-source Traefik Proxy.

features.title: Kubernetes-Native Architecture
features.description: Auto-discovery, Helm charts, and Kubernetes Gateway API support for cloud-native deployments.

features.title: GitOps Configuration
features.description: Declarative API management via Kubernetes Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs).

features.title: API Governance
features.description: API inventory management, versioning, and policy enforcement across API groups.

features.title: Security & IAM
features.description: Granular access control with IAM integrations (Keycloak, Okta, Azure Entra ID), TLS management, and a native Web Application Firewall.

features.title: Observability
features.description: OpenTelemetry integration plus third-party monitoring support (e.g., Treblle).

features.title: Developer Portal
features.description: Self-service API discovery, interactive testing, and credential management for API consumers.

features.title: CI/CD Integration
features.description: Linters, rollback capabilities, and change-impact analysis in CI/CD pipelines.

pros: Built on Traefik Proxy, an open-source project with roughly 50,000 GitHub stars and 3.5+ billion downloads
Kubernetes-native with GitOps-based configuration via CRDs
Integrates with common IAM providers (Keycloak, Okta, Azure Entra ID)
Used by organizations including NASA, Siemens, Dell, eBay, and Expedia
Free trial available to evaluate before purchasing
cons: Pricing for the API Gateway and API Management tiers is not published; requires contacting sales
Full API management capabilities require a paid tier above the free, open-source Traefik Proxy
Kubernetes-native design assumes a Kubernetes-based infrastructure

faqs.question: Is Traefik Hub free?
faqs.answer: Traefik Hub offers a free trial; full pricing for its API Gateway and API Management tiers requires contacting Traefik Labs' sales team. The underlying Traefik Proxy is free and open source.

faqs.question: What is Traefik Hub built on?
faqs.answer: Traefik Hub is built on Traefik Proxy, Traefik Labs' open-source reverse proxy and ingress controller.

faqs.question: What IAM providers does Traefik Hub support?
faqs.answer: Traefik Hub integrates with IAM providers including Keycloak, Okta, and Azure Entra ID.

faqs.question: Does Traefik Hub support GitOps?
faqs.answer: Yes, it uses Kubernetes Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) for declarative, GitOps-based API configuration.

use_cases.title: Kubernetes API Gateway
use_cases.description: Manage and secure APIs running on Kubernetes with native CRDs.
use_cases.audience: Platform engineering and DevOps teams on Kubernetes

use_cases.title: API Governance at Scale
use_cases.description: Enforce versioning and policy across many APIs with an inventory and developer portal.
use_cases.audience: API platform teams at larger organizations

features: Open source ingress controller / reverse proxy
HTTP/2, HTTP/3, TCP, UDP, gRPC support
Advanced security controls (LDAP, JWT, OAuth, OIDC)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Traefik Hub?
paragraphs: Traefik Hub is a Kubernetes-native API management platform from Traefik Labs, built on top of the open-source Traefik Proxy. It lets platform and DevOps teams manage APIs declaratively through Kubernetes Custom Resource Definitions instead of manual UI configuration.

The product sits above Traefik Labs' free, open-source Application Proxy tier, adding an API Gateway layer (WAF, advanced auth, AI/MCP gateway add-ons) and a full API Management layer (developer portal, API mocking, versioning, governance). Traefik Proxy itself has around 50,000 GitHub stars and over 3.5 billion downloads.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Traefik Hub is for
paragraphs: Traefik Hub is aimed at platform engineering and DevOps teams running APIs on Kubernetes who want GitOps-based governance, IAM integration, and a developer portal, and who are willing to work with custom, sales-quoted pricing above the free Traefik Proxy tier.

enrichment.short_description: Hurl is a free, open-source Rust-based command-line tool for running and testing HTTP requests from plain text files.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hurl: free, open-source Rust CLI for running and testing HTTP requests defined in plain text, with CI/CD reporting.

features.title: Plain-Text Request Format
features.description: Defines HTTP requests and expected responses in a simple, readable plain-text syntax.

features.title: Request Chaining
features.description: Runs multiple HTTP requests sequentially, capturing and reusing data between them.

features.title: Response Assertions
features.description: Validates responses using XPath, JSONPath, status codes, headers, and SSL certificate checks.

features.title: Multi-API Support
features.description: Works with REST, GraphQL, SOAP, and other XML/JSON-based APIs.

features.title: CI/CD Reporting
features.description: Generates text, JUnit, TAP, and HTML test reports for pipeline integration.

features.title: Single Binary, No Dependencies
features.description: Distributed as a lightweight Rust binary powered by libcurl, with no runtime dependencies.

pros: Free and open source under the Apache-2.0 license
Around 19,000 GitHub stars, indicating an active user community
Single-binary distribution with no runtime dependencies
Supports HTTP/3 and IPv6 via libcurl
Actively maintained with frequent releases
cons: Command-line/plain-text workflow has a learning curve compared to GUI API clients
No official hosted/cloud service; usage is limited to local or CI execution

faqs.question: Is Hurl free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hurl is free, open-source software licensed under Apache-2.0.

faqs.question: What is Hurl written in?
faqs.answer: Hurl is written in Rust and uses libcurl for HTTP handling, making it fast and dependency-free.

faqs.question: Can Hurl test GraphQL and SOAP APIs?
faqs.answer: Yes, in addition to REST, Hurl supports GraphQL, SOAP, and other XML/JSON-based APIs.

faqs.question: Does Hurl integrate with CI/CD?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hurl can generate text, JUnit, TAP, and HTML reports for CI/CD pipelines.

use_cases.title: API Smoke Testing
use_cases.description: Chain and assert HTTP requests to verify API behavior in CI pipelines.
use_cases.audience: Developers and QA engineers testing REST/GraphQL APIs

use_cases.title: HTTP Scripting
use_cases.description: Script sequences of HTTP requests with data capture between steps, replacing ad hoc curl scripts.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hurl?
paragraphs: Hurl is a free, open-source command-line tool developed by the Orange-OpenSource team for running HTTP requests defined in a simple plain-text format. Built in Rust and powered by libcurl, it ships as a single binary with no runtime dependencies and supports HTTP/3 and IPv6.

Beyond firing individual requests, Hurl can chain multiple requests together, capture data from one response for use in the next, and assert on status codes, headers, and body content using XPath or JSONPath. It has around 19,000 stars on GitHub.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Hurl is for
paragraphs: Hurl suits developers and QA engineers who want a lightweight, scriptable way to test REST, GraphQL, or SOAP APIs from the command line or CI/CD pipelines, without adopting a full GUI API client.

enrichment.short_description: Schemathesis is an open-source, property-based API testing tool that generates tests from OpenAPI and GraphQL schemas.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Schemathesis: open-source property-based testing tool that auto-generates API tests from OpenAPI and GraphQL schemas.

features.title: Property-Based Testing
features.description: Uses Hypothesis to automatically generate diverse test cases from API schemas.

features.title: Schema Validation
features.description: Checks that API responses conform to the OpenAPI specification.

features.title: Schema-Aware Fuzzing
features.description: Generates inputs based on declared data types and constraints to find edge cases.

features.title: CI/CD Integration
features.description: Integrates with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and pytest.

features.title: Reproducible Failures
features.description: Produces minimal reproduction cases and equivalent curl commands for each failure.

features.title: Broad Schema Support
features.description: Supports OpenAPI 2.0, 3.0, 3.1, and GraphQL schemas.

features.title: Extensible via Python Hooks
features.description: Allows custom hooks and configuration for tailored test generation.

pros: Open source and free to use
Research presented at ICSE 2022 found it detected 1.4x-4.5x more defects than other tools tested
Used by engineers at companies including Netflix, SAP, Red Hat, IBM, and JetBrains
Supports both OpenAPI and GraphQL schemas
cons: Paid cloud add-ons (Workbench, TraceCov) don't have public pricing on the main site
Primarily a Python-ecosystem tool, which may require Python familiarity

faqs.question: Is Schemathesis free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Schemathesis tool is free and open source. The company also offers paid cloud add-ons, Workbench and TraceCov.

faqs.question: What schema formats does Schemathesis support?
faqs.answer: Schemathesis supports OpenAPI 2.0, 3.0, and 3.1, as well as GraphQL schemas.

faqs.question: How does Schemathesis generate test cases?
faqs.answer: It uses property-based testing powered by Hypothesis to automatically generate and fuzz test inputs based on your API schema.

faqs.question: Does Schemathesis integrate with CI/CD?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and pytest.

use_cases.title: Automated API Test Generation
use_cases.description: Generate test cases directly from an OpenAPI or GraphQL schema without hand-writing test scripts.

use_cases.title: API Fuzzing in CI
use_cases.description: Run schema-aware fuzz tests in CI/CD pipelines to catch validation bugs before release.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Schemathesis?
paragraphs: Schemathesis is an open-source API testing tool that uses property-based testing to automatically generate test cases from OpenAPI and GraphQL schemas, rather than requiring hand-written test scripts. It's built on the Hypothesis testing library and is written in Python.

Published research presented at ICSE 2022 found that Schemathesis detected 1.4x to 4.5x more defects than other tools when testing real-world APIs. The project is used by engineers at companies including Netflix, SAP, Red Hat, IBM, and JetBrains, and the company behind it also offers paid cloud products, Workbench and TraceCov, for continuous API testing and schema coverage analysis.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Schemathesis is for
paragraphs: Schemathesis suits backend developers and QA engineers who maintain OpenAPI or GraphQL APIs and want automated, schema-driven test generation integrated into CI/CD, without manually writing individual test cases.

name: Dredd
skip_reason: The GitHub repository (apiaryio/dredd) was archived by its owner on 2024-11-08 and is now read-only; the last release was in November 2021. The project appears abandoned/unmaintained.

enrichment.short_description: Convoy is an open-source and cloud webhooks gateway for sending, receiving, and managing webhook events reliably.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Convoy: open-source and cloud webhooks gateway for reliable delivery, retries, and security. Self-hosted plans from $999/month.

features.title: Incoming & Outgoing Webhooks
features.description: Manages both receiving and sending of webhook events through a single gateway.

features.title: Automatic Retries
features.description: Retries failed webhook deliveries automatically for reliability.

features.title: Payload Signing & Verification
features.description: Signs payloads and supports rolling secrets for endpoint security.

features.title: SSRF Protection
features.description: Includes SSRF attack prevention and payload inspection.

features.title: Convoy Playground
features.description: Generates test webhook URLs and analyzes payloads for debugging.

features.title: Control/Data Plane Architecture
features.description: Uses a control and data plane design backed by PostgreSQL for high availability.

features.title: Message Broker Support
features.description: Supports message broker-backed event delivery.

pros: Free, open-source Community edition available for self-hosting
Around 2,800 GitHub stars
14-day free trial for both self-hosted Premium and Cloud options
SOC 2 and GDPR compliant with SSO/SAML support on higher tiers
Built-in SSRF protection and payload signing for webhook security
cons: Community edition lacks portal links, RBAC, and priority support (Premium-only)
Premium self-hosted plan costs $999/month
Enterprise and Cloud pricing require contacting sales
Open-source code is licensed under Elastic License v2.0 rather than a fully permissive license

faqs.question: Is Convoy free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Convoy's Community edition is free and open source for self-hosting. Paid Premium ($999/month) and Enterprise tiers add features like RBAC, SSO, and priority support.

faqs.question: Does Convoy offer a cloud version?
faqs.answer: Yes, Convoy Cloud is a managed offering with a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: What license is Convoy under?
faqs.answer: Convoy's open-source code is licensed under the Elastic License v2.0.

faqs.question: Where is Convoy based?
faqs.answer: Convoy is based in San Francisco, California.

use_cases.title: Outgoing Webhook Delivery
use_cases.description: Reliably send webhook events to customer endpoints with retries and signing.
use_cases.audience: SaaS platforms sending webhooks to customers

use_cases.title: Webhook Ingestion Gateway
use_cases.description: Receive and inspect incoming webhooks with SSRF protection.

use_cases.title: Self-Hosted Event Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Run webhook infrastructure on your own infrastructure for compliance needs.
use_cases.audience: Fintech, healthcare, and other regulated companies

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Convoy?
paragraphs: Convoy is a webhooks gateway for sending, receiving, and managing webhook events reliably. It's available as a free, open-source Community edition for self-hosting, a managed Convoy Cloud service, and paid self-hosted Premium and Enterprise tiers.

The platform includes automatic retries, payload signing with rolling secrets, SSRF attack prevention, and a control/data plane architecture backed by PostgreSQL. Convoy is based in San Francisco and reports SOC 2 and GDPR compliance on its higher tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Convoy is for
paragraphs: Convoy fits engineering teams that need reliable webhook infrastructure, whether self-hosting the free Community edition, paying $999/month for the Premium self-hosted tier, or using the managed Convoy Cloud, including regulated industries like fintech and healthcare that need on-premises deployment options.

enrichment.short_description: Novu is an open-source notification infrastructure platform for multi-channel workflows across email, SMS, push, and chat.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Novu: open-source notification platform for multi-channel workflows (email, SMS, push, chat). Free tier plus plans from $30/month.

features.title: Multi-Channel Delivery
features.description: Sends notifications across 9+ channels including email, in-app inbox, SMS, push, and chat apps like Slack and Microsoft Teams.

features.title: Workflow Orchestration
features.description: Coordinates multi-step, multi-channel notification workflows through a unified API.

features.title: Digest Engine
features.description: Consolidates multiple alerts into a single digest message.

features.title: Prebuilt Inbox Component
features.description: Drops a notification inbox UI into an app with minimal code.

features.title: Block-Based Email Editor
features.description: Provides a React Email-powered visual editor for building notification templates.

features.title: User Preference Management
features.description: Lets end users manage channel preferences and snooze messages.

features.title: Framework Integrations
features.description: Integrates with providers such as Twilio, Resend, Clerk, and Stripe.

pros: Open source with an MIT-licensed core (open-core model) and roughly 39,000 GitHub stars
Generous free tier: 10,000 workflow runs/month forever
Supports 9+ notification channels from one API
Self-hosted deployment available on the Enterprise plan
US and EU data residency options
cons: Paid plans start at $30/month once usage exceeds the free tier's 10,000 workflow runs
Removing Novu branding requires the paid Pro plan or higher
Enterprise features (self-hosting, HIPAA BAA, dedicated SSO) require custom Enterprise pricing

faqs.question: Is Novu free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Novu's Free plan includes 10,000 workflow runs per month at no cost, forever. Paid plans start at $30/month for additional usage and features.

faqs.question: Is Novu open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Novu's core is open source under the MIT license, with additional enterprise features under a commercial license.

faqs.question: Can Novu be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Self-hosted deployment is available on Novu's Enterprise plan.

faqs.question: What channels does Novu support?
faqs.answer: Novu supports email, in-app inbox, SMS, push notifications, and chat channels including Slack and Microsoft Teams.

use_cases.title: Product Notification Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Add multi-channel notifications (email, in-app, push, SMS) to a product without building channel integrations from scratch.

use_cases.title: Alert Digesting
use_cases.description: Consolidate high-frequency alerts into digest notifications to reduce noise.
use_cases.audience: Teams sending frequent transactional or system alerts

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Novu?
paragraphs: Novu is an open-source notification infrastructure platform that lets developers build multi-channel notification workflows for web and mobile apps through a unified API. It supports email, in-app inbox, SMS, push notifications, and chat channels like Slack and Microsoft Teams.

Novu follows an open-core model: its core code is MIT-licensed and open source (around 39,000 GitHub stars), while enterprise features are covered by a commercial license. The Free plan includes 10,000 workflow runs per month forever, with paid Pro, Team, and Enterprise plans for higher volume and additional features like self-hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Novu is for
paragraphs: Novu suits product and engineering teams that need to add multi-channel notifications to an application without building and maintaining separate integrations for each channel provider, from early-stage teams on the free tier to larger organizations needing self-hosted or enterprise deployments.

enrichment.short_description: Knock is a customer messaging infrastructure platform for cross-channel notifications, workflows, and preferences.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Knock: messaging infrastructure for cross-channel notifications with a free Developer plan and paid plans from $250/month.

features.title: Multi-Channel Delivery
features.description: Sends messages via email, SMS, push, chat, and in-app notifications.

features.title: AI-Native Workflow Builder
features.description: Builds messaging workflows using natural-language prompts.

features.title: Data Unification
features.description: Ingests real-time data from warehouses, CDPs, reverse ETL tools, and webhooks.

features.title: Dynamic Segmentation
features.description: Builds audience segments from attributes, events, and relationships.

features.title: AI-Powered Personalization
features.description: Generates tailored email content matching brand layouts.

features.title: Preference Centers
features.description: Gives end users control over channels and send windows.

features.title: Developer Tooling
features.description: Provides a CLI, MCP server, and SDKs for Node, Python, Ruby, Go, Java, .NET, Elixir, and PHP.

features.title: Observability
features.description: Built-in debugging and log streaming for message delivery.

pros: Free Developer plan includes 10K messages/month with unlimited workflows and team members
99.99% uptime guarantee
HIPAA, SOC2, GDPR, and CCPA compliant
Broad SDK support across 8 languages plus an MCP server
Handles hundreds of millions of monthly messages per company claims
cons: Starter plan costs $250/month once free-tier usage limits are exceeded
Enterprise pricing (volume/monthly active user based) requires contacting sales
Branding removal and email client previews require the paid Starter plan or above

faqs.question: Is Knock free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Knock's Developer plan is free and includes 10,000 messages, 500 guide active users, and 500 AI agent credits per month.

faqs.question: How much does Knock cost beyond the free plan?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan costs $250/month with usage-based overage pricing; Enterprise pricing is custom and volume-based.

faqs.question: Where is Knock based?
faqs.answer: Knock is headquartered at 175 Varick St, New York, NY.

faqs.question: What channels does Knock support?
faqs.answer: Knock supports email, SMS, push notifications, chat, and in-app messaging.

faqs.question: Does Knock offer an AI workflow builder?
faqs.answer: Yes, Knock includes an AI-native workflow builder that lets teams create messaging journeys using natural-language prompts.

use_cases.title: Product & Transactional Notifications
use_cases.description: Send cross-channel product and transactional messages without building channel infrastructure from scratch.

use_cases.title: Marketing & Lifecycle Messaging
use_cases.description: Segment audiences from unified customer data and trigger lifecycle messages.

use_cases.title: Regulated-Industry Messaging
use_cases.description: Send compliant notifications under HIPAA, SOC2, GDPR, and CCPA requirements.

features: Volume-based pricing with annual commit discounts

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Knock?
paragraphs: Knock is a customer messaging infrastructure platform for product, marketing, and transactional communications. It unifies customer data from warehouses, CDPs, and webhooks to power cross-channel messaging across email, SMS, push, chat, and in-app notifications, including an AI-native workflow builder that lets teams build messaging journeys from natural-language prompts.

The company is headquartered in New York and reports 11-50 employees. It offers SDKs for eight languages plus an MCP server, and states HIPAA, SOC2, GDPR, and CCPA compliance with a 99.99% uptime guarantee.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Knock is for
paragraphs: Knock fits product and engineering teams building cross-channel notification and messaging systems, from small teams on the free Developer plan to larger or regulated organizations that need enterprise compliance and volume-based pricing.
